'''Lilmit''' was a [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] lieutenant in [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]] of an unknown species with a small size, gray skin, wing-like eyebrows, ridged scalp and webbed hands. He was the owner of the [[Rude Awakening (freighter)|''Rude Awakening'']], a cargo hauler from [[Ord Mantell/Legends|Ord Mantell]] which had been shipping illegal weaponry to war-torn [[Anobis]]. As with many other criminals, he had little or no conscience. During the Anobis conflict, he was a gunrunner and sold weapons to both sides in the conflict.

Following a trip to Ord Mantell, the famous [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] used the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] to board the ''Rude Awakening''. His [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] children [[Darth Caedus|Jacen]] and [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] along with their fellow Jedi [[Zekk]] and [[Anja Gallandro]] then discovered the weapons in storage and blew them up in space. He also worked for another Black Sun lieutenant named [[Czethros]] who attempted to take over the galaxy on that same year through sleeper agents.

While on the spice mining world of [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]], he revealed to Anja that Black Sun had hidden a stash of [[Andris/Legends|andris]] [[Spice/Legends|spice]] in the ocean beneath the polar ice cap of watery [[Dac/Legends|Dac]]. The spice would then be sold to selected clients at the [[Crystal Reef]] resort. Later, after Czethros's plot to take Kessel failed following a [[Battle of Kessel (Black Sun Insurgency)|battle]] with Kessel security forces and Jedi trainees Jaina and [[Lowbacca]], Czethros tried to persuade Lilmit to get him offworld though Lilmit refused. In the end, Lilmit was presumably arrested as with the rest of Black Sun, but he also could have been hired by [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] (as suggested to Lando by [[Anja Gallandro]]).
